entries.objective: Build palantir-mini Claude Code plugin (v2.1.108) + ~/.claude/schemas/ontology extension implementing OSDK 2.0 patterns and Palantir's 5 must-include infra patterns (two-tier actions, edit functions returning Edits[] without commit, OSDK type codegen, 6-phase validation, submission criteria pre-flight) on append-only event log foundation, mirroring Anthropic Managed Agents Session/Harness/Sandbox virtualization, within Claude Code Native Runtime (API-Free, Claude Max X20, max v2.1.108 utilization). Cross-project: kosmos / palantir-math / mathcrew. Promote ~/.claude/research/palantir/ from declarative evidence to executable contract.
userMotivation: 3 projects (kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew) reinvent the same ontology pattern 3x but learnings do not propagate
Foundry-shaped silos without Foundry's actual mechanisms (shared schema registry, propagation enforcement, change audit, capability tokens, OSDK regen)
Multi-runtime fragmentation: ontology trapped in Claude memory blob, Codex/Gemini reinvent
~/.claude/research/palantir/ is read-only evidence, not executable contract — code does not follow patterns
BackwardProp loop broken: kosmos generates blueprints, palantir-math/mathcrew runtime evidence does not feed back
palantir-math data.ts (141KB) approaching OSDK 1.x failure mode — needs OSDK 2.0 migration before pain compounds

constraints.description: API-Free: Claude Max X20 subscription only. No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. No --bare flag, no Managed Agents API, no Bedrock/Vertex.

Claude Code v2.1.108 native runtime only. Maximize: Agent Teams, ToolSearch, PreCompact decision:'block', plugin monitors manifest, managed-settings.d/, /recap, EnterWorktree path, TaskCreated/TaskCompleted hooks.
Multi-runtime: Claude primary writer, Codex/Gemini consume schemas/ontology/ as readers via filesystem. No Codex/Gemini-specific runtime overlays.
Research over codegen: 13-section TechBlueprint is primary deliverable. v0 PoCs are 50-200 LOC each, validate concept not production.

domains.name: Learn Domain (BackwardProp foundation)
evidencePaths: https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/clawhip (event routing to prevent context pollution)

surfaces.name: Claude Code CLI session surface
primitives: Hooks (24 events incl. v2.1.108 PreCompact decision:'block', TaskCreated, TaskCompleted, PostToolUseFailure)
Custom Subagents (.md frontmatter with tools allowlist + maxTurns + isolation + disallowedTools)
MCP Bridge (palantir-mini bridge/mcp-server.ts as Harness API)

surfaces.name: Code editor TypeScript surface
primitives: Branded RID types (ObjectTypeRid, LinkTypeRid, ActionTypeRid, etc.)

researchQuestions.text: Append-only event log foundation: minimum viable schema and atomic-append mechanism for cross-project ontology state under API-Free constraint?
researchQuestions.scope: events.jsonl format, EventEnvelope schema, atomic file lock mechanism, snapshot derivation from log, replay determinism, OMC clawhip pattern adaptation
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Schema published as TypeScript interface, append/read round-trip test passes, replay deterministic across runs

researchQuestions.text: Two-tier action architecture (declarative tier-1 + function-backed tier-2) with read-only edit-function agents enforcing 'returns Edits[] without commit' via disallowedTools?
researchQuestions.scope: Tier-1 declarative rule schema, Tier-2 function signature returning Edits[], SubmissionCriteria type, atomic commit wrapper, agent .md frontmatter pattern (disallowedTools per OMC architect.md)
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Both tiers compile + execute, edit-function agent .md uses disallowedTools deny-list, action-executor agent .md has Write/Edit allowlist, Edits[] round-trips correctly

researchQuestions.text: OSDK 2.0 type codegen pattern (branded RID, separated client/generated, lazy loading) applied to ontology declarations across kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew?
researchQuestions.scope: Branded RID type generator, descender generation entry point, client/generated split enforcement (palantir/osdk-ts pattern), lazy module loading
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Codegen produces valid TS for at least 1 project (palantir-math first), tsc passes, regen is idempotent, separated layers compile independently

researchQuestions.text: MCP bridge as Harness API: which MCP tools to expose for Session/Harness/Sandbox virtualization within Native Runtime?
researchQuestions.scope: MCP server file structure (per OMC bridge/mcp-server.cjs), tool schema for get_ontology / emit_event / apply_edit_function / commit_edits / replay_lineage, integration with .mcp.json, SSE event protocol
researchQuestions.successCriteria: MCP server registers 5 tools, all callable from Claude Code session via mcp__palantir-mini__* tool names, round-trip test passes

researchQuestions.text: 6-phase (or 3-phase minimum) validation pipeline using v2.1.108 hooks (PreCompact decision:'block', PostToolUseFailure, TaskCreated/Completed) with managed-settings.d/ fragments per project?
researchQuestions.scope: Phase definitions (Design/Compile/Runtime/Post-Write minimum), hook handler scripts via thin bootstrapper (per OMC), managed-settings.d/ fragment template, error class taxonomy per phase
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Each phase catches at least 1 distinct error class, hook fires on every Edit(ontology/*), managed-settings.d/ loads cleanly across 3 projects

researchQuestions.text: Submission criteria pre-flight gates as foundation for progressive autonomy?
researchQuestions.scope: SubmissionCriteria type, pre-flight evaluator function, gate failure event emission, integration with action-executor agent
researchQuestions.successCriteria: Failed criteria block action commit + emit event, success criteria allow commit + emit event, both events retrievable from log

researchQuestions.text: Migration sequencing from existing palantir-math (most mature) to mathcrew to kosmos with backward compatibility?
researchQuestions.scope: palantir-math changeContracts.ts (66KB) + capabilities.ts (57KB) + data.ts (141KB) -> palantir-mini equivalents, preserve git history, compat shim during transition
researchQuestions.successCriteria: palantir-math compiles + runs after migration, no breaking changes to existing consumers, clean rollback path

routingDecisions.rationale: Highest density evidence for the 5 must-include patterns + Native Runtime mechanics. palantir-mini-blueprint.md already synthesizes prior research into the v0 architecture.

targets.name: oh-my-claudecode (best practice reference, already deep-dived)
targets.note: Already deep-dived this session — see ~/.claude/research/claude-code/palantir-mini-blueprint.md OMC section. T3 should verify, not re-research.

routingDecisions.rationale: Tier-1 sources (official Anthropic + Palantir + most-starred Korean CC plugin reference)

devCon5Application.phase1_GoldenTables: events.jsonl per project = the durable Golden Table. Snapshots derived from log. ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/ = stable typed interface (the OSDK).
devCon5Application.phase2_OperationalDecisionMaking: Two-tier actions (declarative + function-backed) + submission criteria + 6-phase validation pipeline. Edit functions return Edits[] without commit. Action wraps with atomic commit.
devCon5Application.phase3_AIFirst: MCP bridge exposes operations for Claude/Codex/Gemini. ontology-verifier agent proactively detects drift. Decision lineage 5-dim enables AIP Automate staged review (deferred to future phase).

hypothesisProvisional.winnerCandidate: Hypothesis A: Append-only event log foundation
hypothesisProvisional.rationale: Independent convergence from three references: (1) Anthropic Managed Agents engineering article ('Session = append-only log of everything that happened'), (2) Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim (when/atop_which/through_which/by_whom/with_what), (3) OMC clawhip 'event routing to prevent context pollution'. Three independent sources agree.
hypothesisProvisional.competitor: Hypothesis B: Snapshot-based with StaleObject optimistic concurrency
hypothesisProvisional.competitorEvidence: Palantir validation/README.md StaleObject pattern is real and proven, but is for in-place mutation, not stateless harness model. Adversarial scenario: two parallel writers create lost updates.

inputsConsulted: ontology-state/decision-log.json (T1 decomposition, 7 RQs, provisional H-A winner)
ontology-state/source-map.json (T2+T3, 12 sources, 30 claims, 6 contradictions all either resolved or deliberately architectural)
ontology-state/world-model.json (T4, 28 primitives, 5 domains populated, overallForwardPropStatus=healthy, overallBackwardPropStatus=healthy, DDD/DRY/OCP/PECS all applied)
ontology-state/scenarios.json (T5+T6, 3 hypotheses, 12 scenarios, 11-dim scoring, 2 revision rounds, provisional H-A + H-C winner)
ontology-state/eval-results.json (T7+T8+T9, both prototypes buildStatus=success, H-A 10/10 clean, H-B 10/10 formal with 4 classified FailureModes)

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Low-tier dependency
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Critical claims anchor on tier-1 sources: Anthropic Managed Agents (official), Palantir Decision Lineage / mutations / validation / functions (official), Claude Code hooks+plugins-reference (official), Palantir OSDK v2 Edits (official). Only src-ext-proper-lockfile is tier-3-vendor-blogs, and it is not critical (fs.mkdir fallback is the production path and was empirically validated in T7).

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Unresolved contradictions
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Scenario contradictionStatus audit: S-HA-BASE=none, S-HA-BEST=none, S-HA-WORST=evidence-gap-driven (T7 fills), S-HA-ADV=evidence-gap-driven (T7 fills and empirically resolved with 0 lost events / 2000), S-HB-BASE=unresolvable (classified as irreconcilable architectural fork — H-B is NOT the winner, so this preserves rather than blocks the decision), all remaining scenarios either none or resolved. source-map.json contradictions 01-05 resolved, contra-06 (StaleObject in append-only) empirically resolved by T7 adversarial race (0 lost events, sequence counter works as version vector per gap-01).

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Missing scenario link
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Recommendation winner H-A is backed by 4 scenarios (S-HA-BASE, S-HA-BEST, S-HA-WORST, S-HA-ADV). H-C co-winner backed by 4 scenarios (S-HC-*). scenarioIds count per winning path >= 1 (actually 8).

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Missing risk link
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: strongestContradictionsFlagged in scenarios.json lists 3 flagged risks (flag-01 S-HA-ADV race, flag-02 H-B LEARN violation, flag-03 Bun compat). criticalRisks surfaced here and propagate to reporter. riskIds >= 1.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Stale evidence
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: source-map.json: 0 claims with freshnessStatus='stale'. Distribution: most claims are 'current', two are 'aging' (proper-lockfile 2020, Palantir Decision Lineage 2024). 0/30 stale << 50% threshold.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Blurred provenance
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: All claims tagged with provenance: 'official' (28) / 'synthesis' (1) / 'inference' (1). clm-ext-24 is the single inference claim and explicitly flagged as inferred with confidence=0.75. No mixed tagging.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Missing win rationale
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: winRationale is specific and non-generic: three independent evaluation axes (architectural convergence + empirical superiority + ontology fit), each backed by enumerated evidence paths.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: No alternatives
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Alternatives preserved on record: H-B (snapshot+StaleObject) is the formal competitor, scored with 4 scenarios, and kept in the decision log with empirical defeat documentation. alternatives[] >= 1.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Insufficient evidence
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Winning scenario S-HA-BASE evidenceSufficiency='sufficient'. S-HA-BEST=partial, S-HA-WORST=partial, S-HA-ADV=partial — but partial is not insufficient per rubric, and T7+T9 empirical results upgraded the partial scenarios by filling the Bun compatibility gap and the 2-writer race gap.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Missing reversal conditions
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: whatWouldChangeDecision has 3 concrete reversal conditions listed below (Bun+fs.mkdir fallback failure, H-C integration failure, CRDT-based MCP pattern emergence). >= 1.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: D/L/A classification missing
rejectionCriteriaApplied.evidence: ontology-state/eval-results.json evalSuites[1].failureModes[0]: 24.2% empirical update loss rate (484/2000 in run1, 56-484 across 3 runs). LEARN domain cannot be reliably populated from a lossy substrate — prim-learn-01 AppendOnlyEventLog and prim-learn-04 LineageReplay are structurally impossible on H-B. Also confirmed by scenarios.json S-HB-BASE contradictionStatus='unresolvable' (irreconcilable LEARN centrality violation).
rejectionCriteriaApplied.decisionImpact: This trigger is NOT a pipeline defect — it is the empirical confirmation of H-B's architectural defeat. Winning hypothesis H-A populates all 5 domains first-class per world-model.json (DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/SECURITY/LEARN, 28 primitives, overallForwardPropStatus=healthy). R11 on H-B strengthens, not weakens, the recommendation.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: DevCon 5 principles not applied
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: world-model.json devCon5PrinciplesApplied=['DDD','DRY','OCP','PECS'] with per-primitive findings documented. H-A devcon5Alignment: DDD PASS / DRY PASS / OCP PASS / PECS PASS (scenarios.json hypothesis H-A). All 28 primitives have per-principle analysis in world-model.json.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: ForwardProp/BackwardProp broken
rejectionCriteriaApplied.evidence: ontology-state/eval-results.json evalSuites[1].failureModes[1]+[3]: TOCTOU window non-atomic in snapshot write path (ACTION critical); no lineage across versions — prior states irrecoverable after overwrite (DATA high). BackwardProp path from H-B substrate to ontology is structurally broken: replay_lineage cannot reconstruct history from a substrate that only holds current snapshot state. Also confirmed by scenarios.json S-HB-BASE architectureImplications 'LOSS: BackwardProp path is crippled'.
rejectionCriteriaApplied.decisionImpact: Same posture as R11: triggers on H-B, confirms H-A win, not a pipeline defect. H-A's own ForwardProp+BackwardProp paths are healthy per world-model.json (overallForwardPropStatus=healthy, overallBackwardPropStatus=healthy).

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Prototype build failure
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: Both prototypes buildStatus=success. H-A: 5 pass / 0 fail / 2247 expect() calls / tsc clean. H-B: 5 pass / 0 fail / 26 expect() calls / tsc clean. Neither has buildStatus=fail.

rejectionCriteriaApplied.name: Eval pass rate below threshold
rejectionCriteriaApplied.note: H-A passRate=1.0 (10/10), H-B passRate=1.0 (10/10). Both above 0.5 threshold. H-B has 4 classified failureModes (2 critical LEARN+ACTION, 2 high LOGIC+DATA) which the eval-runner has explicitly documented with affectedCases and severity — satisfies the FailureMode analysis requirement even if passRate had been below threshold. H-A has 0 failureModes.

rejectionCriteriaSummary.winnerRulesClean: All 15 rules clean for winning path (H-A + H-C). R11 and R13 triggers on H-B are structural confirmations of H-B's defeat, not pipeline defects.
rejectionCriteriaSummary.rationale: When R11/R13 trigger on the LOSING hypothesis and the eval-runner has classified failureModes that explain WHY the loss is structural rather than load-dependent, the triggers are evaluator signals of pipeline HEALTH: they confirm that the eval gate is discriminating real architectural quality, not rubber-stamping.

triggerChecks.passRateDiffExplanation: H-A 1.0 and H-B 1.0 formal — 0% diff on raw pass rate. Qualitative divergence is captured via failureModes analysis, not via pass-rate debate.
triggerChecks.dlaExplanation: No misclassification. The ontologist's transition-zone calls (LinkType/InterfaceType/DerivedProperty/Reducer -> LOGIC) were applied rigorously and accepted by the simulator without re-debate (scenarios.json simulatorAudit.transitionZoneRespected).
triggerChecks.newFailureTypeExplanation: H-B's failure modes (TOCTOU non-atomic write, lossy substrate, missing lineage) were all predicted by S-HB-ADV and S-HB-WORST scenarios BEFORE the prototype. Empirical results confirmed simulator predictions. No surprise failure mode requiring debate.

debateProtocol.note: Zero rounds used. The evidence chain (scenarios prediction -> prototype confirmation -> eval classification) is coherent and the winner is decisively clear across all 11 evaluation dimensions + empirical race results + architectural convergence. No defense/revision cycle needed.

entries.coWinnerNote: H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) is ORTHOGONAL to H-A vs H-B — it runs ON TOP of the event-log substrate, not instead of it. scenarios.json provisionalWinner.recommendationToEvaluator: 'H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) runs ON TOP of H-A and is complementary, not competing.' Both accepted.
entries.formalCompetitor: H-B (Snapshot + StaleObject) — preserved for record with empirical defeat documentation (484 lost updates / 2000 in run1, 24.2% loss rate, TOCTOU structural, no BackwardProp path)
entries.winRationale: H-A (Append-Only Event Log Foundation) is the decisive winner across 3 independent evaluation axes: (1) ARCHITECTURAL CONVERGENCE — three independent references (Anthropic Managed Agents 'Session=append-only log', Palantir Decision Lineage 5-dim, OMC clawhip event routing) all converge on the append-only log pattern; (2) EMPIRICAL SUPERIORITY — H-A prototype passes 2-writer adversarial race at 2x1000 events with 0 duplicate sequences, 0 torn writes, 0 lost events (reproducible across 2 runs) versus H-B losing 484/2000 updates (24.2%) with reproducibility confirming TOCTOU is structural not load-dependent; (3) ONTOLOGY FIT — H-A populates all 5 domains first-class per world-model's 28 primitives (DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/SECURITY/LEARN all overallForwardPropStatus=healthy and overallBackwardPropStatus=healthy) while H-B cannot reliably populate LEARN (R11) and has structurally broken BackwardProp (R13). R11 and R13 triggered for H-B confirm rather than invalidate the pipeline: they are the evaluator signals that H-B lost on architectural grounds, which is the pipeline's job to detect.
entries.architecturalDecision: ACCEPT H-A as palantir-mini v0 state foundation. ACCEPT H-C (3-phase validation pipeline: Design+Compile fused / Runtime / Post-Write) as the validation depth that runs on top of H-A. H-B is preserved as the formal competitor of record with full empirical defeat documentation for BackwardProp audit purposes.

criticalRisks.description: Bun compatibility for proper-lockfile is tier-3 inference (clm-ext-24, confidence 0.75). H-A prototype-a resolved this empirically by using fs.mkdir fallback path and proved 0 lost events / 2000 — but proper-lockfile itself was not directly tested under Bun in T7. For v1 integration the plugin should either commit to fs.mkdir fallback as primary or empirically verify proper-lockfile under Bun.
criticalRisks.mitigation: T7 already proved fs.mkdir-only path works; use that as the production path in v1 and defer proper-lockfile to optional optimization.

criticalRisks.description: Cross-project shared schemas write coordination — 3 projects (kosmos/palantir-math/mathcrew) share ~/.claude/schemas/ontology/. Claude Code is declared sole writer (constraint.boundary) and git handles coordination between parallel Claude sessions — but this assumption must hold at deployment time.
criticalRisks.mitigation: gap-03 resolved this architecturally: git branches coordinate parallel sessions and per-project events.jsonl lockfile handles per-project atomic writes. No new mechanism needed for v0.

criticalRisks.description: proper-lockfile last published 2020 (5 years old, aging). Still 9.27M weekly downloads but maintainer activity is low.
criticalRisks.mitigation: fs.mkdir zero-dep fallback is already the empirically proven path. Consider making fallback the only path in v1.

criticalRisks.description: BackwardProp persistence gap (world-model backwardProp.gaps steps 4+5): refinement proposals persistence not yet implemented. H-A Phase III (AI-First) requires this loop to close for the full DevCon 5 journey.
criticalRisks.mitigation: Deferred to post-v0. Phase I+II are self-contained and do not require Phase III closure.

whatWouldChangeDecision: If a v1 integration test shows fs.mkdir fallback is insufficient under 10+ concurrent writers AND proper-lockfile is empirically broken under Bun, revisit H-A's atomic append mechanism (application-level retry-on-conflict or alternative lockfile strategy).
If H-C (3-phase validation pipeline) fails integration in v1 due to PostToolUse hook latency burnout, reconsider validation monitoring scope (retreat to 2-phase or move to async:true).
If a new MCP server pattern emerges that makes snapshot-based architecture viable for LEARN (e.g., CRDT-based merging with cross-version history), reconsider H-B or H-A+snapshot hybrid.
If palantir-math migration reveals EventEnvelope variant gaps that require breaking schema bumps before v0 stabilizes, revisit gap-04 and consider versioned events.jsonl.
If cross-project write coordination (gap-03) proves insufficient because Codex/Gemini become writers not readers, reintroduce multi-writer coordination mechanism.
entries.nextSpawnRecommendation: Spawn reporter-blueprint for T11 (TechBlueprint JSON per schemas/types.ts) AND reporter-report for T12 (13-section markdown report) IN PARALLEL. Both depend only on T10 accepted and both can read all existing ontology-state/*.json without coordination. Expected parallel runtime: ~20-30 min on Claude Max X20.
pipelineHealthObservations: Evidence chain coherence: scenarios predicted H-B TOCTOU weakness (S-HB-ADV) BEFORE T8 prototype, and T8 confirmed empirically at 484 lost updates. This is the eval gate working as designed.

Transition zone discipline: LinkType/Interface/Derived/Reducer all classified as LOGIC by ontologist, accepted by simulator, and preserved through prototyper and eval-runner. No re-litigation downstream.

First session to touch all 5 domains first-class: palantir-mini's LEARN+SECURITY domains are real primitives, not N/A placeholders. 28 primitives across 5 domains is the densest ontology in the kosmos session history.

H-B preservation as formal competitor is working: even though H-B lost, the pipeline kept H-B scored, prototyped, eval'd, and documented. This satisfies R8 (No alternatives) AND provides BackwardProp material for future sessions considering snapshot-based alternatives.

validations: All RQs have D/L/A domain tags
Routing decisions for T2 and T3 specified
Provisional hypothesis identified with 3-source convergence rationale
2 rules triggered on losing hypothesis H-B (R11, R13) — pipeline health signal, not defect
0 rules triggered on winning hypothesis H-A
0 debate rounds used (out of max 2) — evidence chain is coherent
gateResult=ACCEPT set at top level and inside entry
winRationale specific and non-generic (3 axes, enumerated evidence)
criticalRisks enumerated with severity and mitigation (>= 1)
Formal competitor H-B preserved with empirical defeat documentation
